svg.text: Data Structures
I. Structure: Definition & Key Concepts
A Structure is an advanced data type that groups different kinds of data into a single custom
data type. Each Structure has a unique name that identifies it. Fields within a Structure are
called Members, each with a unique field name and a data type.
Structures support nesting — a Structure can contain other Structures and Structure Lists.
Pass by Reference: Structure data in node graphs is passed by reference, similar to Lists.
II. Structure: Creating & Editing
Access Advanced Data Management from the editor menu to open the Structure Editing tab.
View existing Structures, add new ones, define Members with names and types. Once created,
Structures are available in Server Node Graphs, Custom Variables, and Node Graph Variables.
III. Structure Node Graph Nodes
Assemble Structure
Node Type: Operation
Fill in values for each Member parameter to generate Structure data. Select the Structure type
via the Generic selection button first. All Members of the chosen Structure appear as inputs.
Split Structure
Node Type: Operation
Pass in a Structure to obtain the values of each Member. Select the Structure type via Generic.
Modify Structure
Node Type: Execution
Edit selected Member values within a Structure. Use the dropdown to choose which Members
to modify — unchecked Members retain their original values.
IV. Dictionary: Definition & Key Concepts
A Dictionary is a data structure representing a set of key-value mapping relationships.
Each Dictionary maps data type A (Key) to data type B (Value). Keys must be unique.
Type Determination: A Dictionary's type is defined by both its Key and Value types.
Pass by Reference: Dictionaries are passed by reference in node graphs.
Unordered: Key-Value Pairs are not stored in order. Do not rely on sequence.
Available Key Types: Entity, GUID, Integer, String, Factions, Prefab ID, Configuration ID.
Value types include all basic types, list types, and custom Structures.
V. Dictionary Node Graph Nodes
Execution Nodes
Set or Add Key Value Pair: Add a new pair or update an existing key's value.
Remove Key Value Pair by Key: Remove an entry from the dictionary by its key.
Clear Dictionary: Remove all entries from the dictionary.
Sort Dictionary by Key / by Value: Sort the dictionary entries.
Query Nodes
Query Dictionary Length: Get the number of key-value pairs.
Query Dictionary Value by Key: Retrieve a value using its key.
Get List of Keys / Get List of Values: Extract all keys or all values as a list.
Query If Dictionary Contains Specific Key / Value: Check for existence.
Operation Nodes
Create Dictionary: Create a new empty dictionary of a specified key-value type.
Assembly Dictionary: Build a dictionary by specifying key-value pairs directly.
Generic Pin Usage
Both Structure and Dictionary types use Generic Pins. Select the specific type from the generic
dropdown before the node can be fully defined.
Related Documentation:
AI Generated, based on Craftspeople Academy. Version: Luna 7
